decisions.relationship_evidence: Compared self-reported STIs with biomarker-confirmed diagnoses and found substantial differential underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct diagnostic comparison of self-report against biological testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarked self-reported ring adherence against plasma and residual-ring biomarkers, demonstrating inflated adherence reports.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct validation of self-report using two objective adherence measures.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compared reported sexual debut with STI and Y-chromosome biomarkers and identified underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Behavior reports were assessed against biological evidence of sexual exposure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used hepatitis C antibodies to reveal likely injection-drug-use underreporting among participants denying injection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Biological findings were used to diagnose behavioral self-report error.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Modeled substance-use measurement error and showed that ignoring it materially underestimated exposure-outcome associations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly demonstrates a Bayesian method using sensitivity and specificity estimates for imperfect measures.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied a double list experiment to reduce social-desirability bias and contrasted estimates with face-to-face reports.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to condom-use measurement among sex workers in Senegal.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Systematically pooled discrepancies between self-reported known HIV status and biological or clinical knowledge proxies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Meta-analysis directly quantifies underreporting of known HIV-positive status.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Measured network ties omitted during free-listing but recovered by prompting and quantified their effects on network measures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of omission bias and its analytic consequences.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assessed forgetting, embarrassment, and perceived irrelevance as mechanisms of underreporting sexual behavior.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Applied underreporting assessment to individualized blood-donor screening communication.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly adjusted vaccination-intent survey responses for possible reporting bias among vaccine-reluctant respondents.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Reporting-bias adjustment was applied to COVID-19 vaccination-intent data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compared self-reported condom use with Y-chromosome DNA and found 50% discordance and underreported condomless sex.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct biomarker validation of reported sexual behavior.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used neonatal phosphatidylethanol to confirm maternal alcohol exposure and found self-report underreporting common.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Self-reported alcohol use was checked against an objective biomarker.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Experimentally estimated contact-reporting decline with recall delay and evaluated age and memory-aid effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct quantitative evaluation of recall-related reporting loss in contact tracing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used qualitative analysis to investigate why measured and self-reported weight loss diverge during TB screening.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete assessment of symptom underreporting in HIV-associated TB screening.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compared AUDIT-C reports with phosphatidylethanol and found 46% underreporting among biomarker-positive participants.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct biomarker assessment of unhealthy-alcohol-use self-report.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and validates a causal framework combining targeted testing with randomized surveillance to debias local prevalence estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and held-out validation of surveillance extrapolation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrated sentinel-facility, household, and serological data to calibrate a model estimating underreported COVID-19 burden.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete sentinel-data calibration application in Burkina Faso and Madagascar.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluated hospital screening incidences as proxies for general-population SARS-CoV-2 incidence using correlations and temporal comparisons.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct performance evaluation of a hospital-based surveillance proxy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used a 20-laboratory sentinel surveillance study to describe hepatitis C testing and positivity trends in English sexual-health services.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis C descriptive surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used sentinel-site data to describe rectal chlamydia and gonorrhea trends in San Francisco.
Conducted repeated HIV sentinel serosurveillance among pregnant women to estimate prevalence trends from 2001 to 2007.
Established sentinel surveillance in five hospitals and described the etiologic epidemiology of acute viral hepatitis.
Conducted prospective nationwide multicenter surveillance to describe candidemia incidence, species, resistance, and risk factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete descriptive surveillance of bloodstream Candida infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used ACCESS sentinel data to report and compare chlamydia testing and positivity rates in sexual-health services.
The title explicitly reports nationwide sentinel surveillance of bloodstream Candida infections across 40 hospitals.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multicenter candidemia surveillance application identified in the title.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzed repeated sentinel surveys to describe HIV prevalence trends across multiple priority populations in China.
Analyzed CDC sentinel surveillance isolates to describe regional and temporal antimicrobial resistance in Helicobacter pylori.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete antimicrobial-resistance surveillance application in Alaska.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used prospective surveillance in two Ecuadorian cities to describe influenza-like-illness viruses, strains, and temporal patterns.
Integrated acute, recent, and drug-resistant HIV testing into sentinel counseling-and-testing surveillance and described resulting cases.
Analyzed seven years of Thai sentinel-site specimens to describe influenza frequency, seasonality, and circulating strains.
Used five annual sentinel-site surveys to describe HIV, syphilis, and behavioral trends among men who have sex with men.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete integrated behavioral and serological HIV/STI surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimated pharyngeal chlamydia and gonorrhea positivity among men screened at five San Francisco sentinel sites.
Collected canine fecal samples across five communities to conduct sentinel surveillance for zoonotic parasites.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete animal-sentinel application for zoonotic infection surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzed eight years of sentinel-site specimens to describe influenza and other respiratory viruses in Côte d’Ivoire.
Used China’s national influenza-like-illness sentinel system to identify and characterize mild-to-moderate A/H7N9 cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete case-detection application of national sentinel surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzed community testing results to describe clinical and epidemiological features of acute Mycoplasma pneumoniae infection.
Combined several sentinel surveillance systems to describe Corsica’s 2012–2013 influenza-like-illness outbreak.
Conducted six-site sentinel surveillance to estimate influenza prevalence, strains, age distribution, and seasonality in Burkina Faso.
Used a clinic-based sentinel system to estimate STI diagnosis and incidence trends among gay and bisexual men.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete STI trend-surveillance application in British Columbia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzed China’s first severe-acute-respiratory-infection sentinel system to describe influenza epidemiology, virology, and clinical features.
Prospectively screened specimens from a community sentinel network to describe enterovirus-D68 detection and epidemiology.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete community sentinel-surveillance application for EV-D68.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used two long-term sentinel laboratories to estimate the occurrence of enterotoxigenic and Shiga-toxin-producing E. coli.
Established laboratory-supported surveillance in ten Haitian hospitals to estimate cholera proportions and detect serotypes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hospital sentinel-surveillance application for cholera.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performed microbiological surveillance among Syrian migrants and described unusual organisms and antimicrobial resistance.
Used laboratory-based surveillance across sentinel hospitals to describe Candida species and antifungal susceptibility.
Enrolled SARI and acute-febrile-illness inpatients at three hospitals to characterize influenza prevalence and seasonality.
Applied a structured sampling framework across 455 sentinel hospitals to estimate carbapenemase-producing Enterobacteriaceae occurrence.
Conducted sentinel-based community, medical-record, and laboratory surveillance to estimate norovirus-associated diarrhea incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete norovirus incidence-surveillance application in Shanghai.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used enhanced sentinel surveillance in 35 hospitals to describe E. coli bacteraemia sources, risks, and resistance.
Implemented surveillance at five Zimbabwean sentinel sites and estimated gonococcal antimicrobial susceptibility and resistance.
Used Switzerland’s Sentinella network to estimate acute-gastroenteritis consultations, management, and work absenteeism.
Analyzed long-running sentinel bacteraemia surveillance to describe bloodstream infections and antimicrobial-resistance trends in Malawi.
Used a sentinel network to describe influenza positivity, types, age patterns, and seasonality in the Central African Republic.
Analyzed ACCESS sentinel data to estimate hepatitis C testing, retesting, incidence, and associated factors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis C surveillance application among people with HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used microbiological surveillance data to characterize colonization and unusual organisms among Eritrean migrants.
Used prospective active sentinel surveillance to describe rotavirus burden, seasonality, and genotype distribution in hospitalized children.
Analyzed German primary-care sentinel data to describe age-specific herpes-zoster trends over time.
Established a hospital sentinel network to estimate childhood bacterial-meningitis burden, pathogens, serotypes, and resistance.
Conducted a prospective study in four sentinel hospitals to describe rotavirus prevalence, seasonality, severity, and genotypes.
Used hospital-based acute-febrile-illness sentinel surveillance to detect and confirm Zika infections in Uganda.
Analyzed Ethiopian influenza sentinel-site data to describe strains, positivity, age patterns, and seasonality.
Used prospective hospital-based sentinel specimens to describe human-astrovirus prevalence and epidemiologic factors in children.
Reported congenital-rubella-syndrome cases identified during the first phase of surveillance at five Indian sentinel sites.
Analyzed CanTravNet sentinel data to describe infectious-disease patterns among migrants presenting in Canada.
Applied child-development-center sentinel surveillance to estimate soil-transmitted-helminth prevalence and infection intensity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete parasitic-disease surveillance application among preschool children.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzed reports from sentinel hospitals in 26 countries to describe pediatric bacterial-meningitis pathogens and vaccine-type trends.
Used active hospital surveillance to estimate influenza detection, seasonality, and associations among severe respiratory cases.
Sampled Canadian sentinel flocks to estimate enteric-pathogen prevalence and antimicrobial-resistance patterns using two matrices.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete food-animal pathogen and resistance surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used an eleven-hospital sentinel network to estimate childhood bacterial-meningitis burden, pathogens, serotypes, and resistance.
Used healthcare workers as a monitored sentinel population to characterize COVID-19 and assess hospital prevention measures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete occupational sentinel-population application during COVID-19.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied a national sentinel testing strategy to compare longitudinal SARS-CoV-2 IgG positivity after two vaccines.
Used Australian enhanced sentinel surveillance data to estimate hepatitis C incidence trends among gay and bisexual men.
Used California sentinel outpatient sites to describe SARS-CoV-2 and other respiratory-virus detections and associated characteristics.
Established prospective surveillance in a sentinel hospital to describe influenza A positivity, subtypes, and temporal patterns.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hospital influenza surveillance application in Lahore.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used sentinel primary-care-clinic data to describe hepatitis C testing volume and test-yield trends from 2009 to 2019.
Analyzed a national sentinel clinical network to estimate bacterial-STI incidence trends during PrEP use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete STI incidence-surveillance application among PrEP users.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implemented surveillance at five sentinel hospitals to describe the pathogen etiology of acute febrile illness.
Describes a facility-based maternity surveillance network but does not identify or explicitly apply sentinel surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Facility-based surveillance alone is insufficient evidence for this exact sentinel-surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used Dutch sentinel hospital data to compare Clostridioides difficile incidence and patient characteristics across COVID-19 waves.
Collected mosquitoes and bats nationwide to estimate Japanese-encephalitis-virus prevalence and geographic risk through sentinel surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vector and animal sentinel-surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied a new nationwide sentinel scheme to monitor geographic and temporal distributions of Clostridioides difficile types.
Uses retrospective antenatal serological surveillance as a population proxy but never identifies the design as sentinel surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Proxy serosurveillance is not explicit evidence for the exact sentinel-surveillance concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses repeated blood-donor serosurveillance to estimate underreporting but does not explicitly describe a sentinel surveillance design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Blood-donor proxy sampling alone does not establish this exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzed EuroTravNet sentinel-network reports to describe pandemic-related changes in travel-associated infectious diseases.
Used nationwide hospital sentinel sites to describe confirmed cholera distribution, risk factors, and antimicrobial resistance.
Analyzed cases detected through India’s sentinel congenital-rubella surveillance to describe cardiac-defect profiles and mortality.
Conducted enhanced SARI surveillance within a national sentinel system to describe pathogens, outcomes, and resource use.
Used outpatient sentinel specimens and hospital data to describe RSV seasonality, lineages, age distribution, and severity.
Analyzed a sentinel mortality system using verbal autopsy and postmortem testing to describe undercounted COVID-19 deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 mortality-surveillance application in Lusaka.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied active sentinel surveillance to describe pediatric bacterial-pneumonia cases, serotypes, and antimicrobial susceptibility.
Collected respiratory specimens at sentinel sites to characterize circulating pathogens in the Middle East and Latin America.
Analyzed 16 years of specimens from twelve Pakistani sentinel sites to describe influenza positivity and subtype patterns.
Combined CARI sentinel data with non-sentinel sources to describe parainfluenza positivity and demographic and temporal patterns.
Used linked records from 58 ACCESS sentinel clinics to quantify the hepatitis C care cascade among OAT recipients.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis C care-cascade surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used nationwide ILI/SARI sentinel surveillance to describe RSV prevalence, subtypes, age patterns, and seasonality in Ethiopia.
Used an Australian sentinel system to estimate hepatitis B susceptibility and subsequent vaccination across priority populations.
Analyzed Poland’s SENTINEL system to compare circulating influenza and influenza-like viruses across two seasons.
Used Denmark’s primary-care sentinel system to describe year-round respiratory-virus activity and symptom patterns.
Analyzed eight years of cases from a Colombian sentinel hospital to describe pediatric respiratory pathogens and outcomes.
Used 15 years of Ugandan national sentinel data to describe RSV prevalence, coinfections, seasonality, and risk factors in older adults.
